author: Besting
text: REVISED 2026-06-06: landing config is the SOLE authority for what runs + where it syncs; changed ONLY by user command (install). ALL config is potential RCE — no 'safe data auto-flows' carve-out (drop the where/what reframe). Defaults are NOT code-level: a fresh landing is SEEDED at prime from the app default-config/ folder (see new seed task), so 'built-in' is only the serde fallback for absent fields. Read path stays CLI>XDG>landing>serde-fallback. spec bl-2e26 §0/§4.

author: Aurally
text: Stale instruction (2026-06-06): this ball says 'Keep merge semantics ... and id_scheme' — but id_scheme was ALREADY REMOVED by bl-f74d (main 919584c, 'Remove the id_scheme config knob'). User decision: id_scheme is NOT a config field; the scheme is fixed (IdScheme::default() = bl-+4 hex), custom generation is a create/pre plugin. So do NOT re-add it. After bl-f74d, EffectiveConfig has ONE field (branch); this ball DROPs branch and ADDs tasks_branch — net result should be EffectiveConfig { tasks_branch } ONLY (no id_scheme). The id::IdScheme struct stays (generator, used by mutate create + change-token) but is not config and has no serde derives (bl-f74d dropped them). Spec docs/architecture.md §4 already reflects this.
